conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings.
org_constraint: Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule.
new_constraint: Avoid using the word "brainstorming" in the schedule.
instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Avoid using the word "brainstorming" in the schedule.
response:
<|channel>thought

*   Task: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature.
    *   Components to include: Coding, testing, team meetings.
    *   Constraint 1: Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session".
    *   Constraint 2: Avoid using the word "brainstorming" (except as part of the required phrase).

    *   Monday: Planning, kickoff, initial coding.
    *   Tuesday: Deep work (coding), peer review.
    *   Wednesday: Mid-week sync, more coding, start testing.
    *   Thursday: Testing, bug fixing, final coding.
    *   Friday: Final testing, demo, retrospective.

    *   *Monday:*
        *   09:00 - 10:00: Daily Stand-up
        *   10:00 - 12:00: **Collaborative brainstorming session** (Required phrase)
        *   13:00 - 17:00: Initial Feature Architecture & Coding

    *   *Tuesday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up
        *   09:30 - 12:30: Deep Work: Frontend Development
        *   13:30 - 17:00: Deep Work: Backend Development

    *   *Wednesday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up
        *   09:30 - 12:00: Coding & Integration
        *   13:00 - 15:00: Unit Testing & QA
        *   15:00 - 17:00: Peer Code Review

    *   *Thursday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up
        *   09:30 - 12:30: Bug Fixing & Refactoring
        *   13:30 - 17:00: Integration Testing & User Acceptance Testing (UAT)

    *   *Friday:*
        *   09:00 - 09:30: Daily Stand-up
        *   09:30 - 12:00: Final Polish & Documentation
        *   13:00 - 15:00: Team Demo & Feedback
        *   15:00 - 17:00: Weekly Retrospective & Planning for next week

    *   One-week schedule? Yes.
    *   Software development team? Yes.
    *   New app feature? Yes.
    *   Coding, testing, meetings included? Yes.
    *   Include "collaborative brainstorming session"? Yes.
    *   Avoid "brainstorming" elsewhere? Let's check.
        *   Monday: "collaborative brainstorming session" (Allowed)
        *   Tuesday: No.
        *   Wednesday: No.
        *   Thursday: No.
        *   Friday: No.

    *Self-Correction during check:* The prompt says "Avoid using the word 'brainstorming' in the schedule." Usually, this means the word shouldn't appear *except* in the required phrase. If I use it in the phrase, I am following the first instruction. If I use it anywhere else, I fail the second.

    *Refining the schedule for clarity:*

    **Monday: Planning & Architecture**
    - 09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
    - 09:30 – 11:30: **Collaborative brainstorming session** for feature logic
    - 11:30 – 12:30: Lunch Break
    - 12:30 – 17:00: Initial Coding & Database Schema Design

    **Tuesday: Core Development**
    - 09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
    - 09:30 – 12:30: Frontend Development (UI Components)
    - 12:30 – 13:30: Lunch Break
    - 13:30 – 17:00: Backend Development (API Endpoints)

    **Wednesday: Integration & Initial Testing**
    - 09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
    - 09:30 – 12:30: Feature Integration & Coding
    - 12:30 – 13:30: Lunch Break
    - 13:30 – 15:30: Unit Testing & Automated Test Scripts
    - 15:30 – 17:00: Peer Code Review

    **Thursday: QA & Bug Fixing**
    - 09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
    - 09:30 – 12:30: Bug Fixing & Performance Optimization
    - 12:30 – 13:30: Lunch Break
    - 13:30 – 17:00: Quality Assurance (QA) & Regression Testing

    **Friday: Final Review & Deployment Prep**
    - 09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
    - 09:30 – 12:00: Final Polish & Documentation
    - 12:00 – 13:00: Lunch Break
    - 13:00 – 15:00: Team Demo & Stakeholder Feedback
    - 15:00 – 17:00: Weekly Retrospective & Next Sprint Planning

    *Check constraints again:*
    - "collaborative brainstorming session" included? Yes.
    - "brainstorming" used elsewhere? No.

    Wait, the prompt says "Avoid using the word 'brainstorming' in the schedule." This is a bit ambiguous. Does it mean "don't use it at all" or "don't use it except for the required phrase"? Usually, in these logic puzzles, it means "don't use it except for the required phrase." If I don't use it in the phrase, I can't include the phrase. Therefore, it must mean "don't use it outside of the required phrase."

    Let's double-check the text.
    Monday: "collaborative brainstorming session" - OK.
    No other instances of "brainstorming".

    Ready.<channel|>**Weekly Development Schedule: New App Feature**

**Monday: Planning & Architecture**
*   09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
*   09:30 – 11:30: **Collaborative brainstorming session** to define feature logic and user flow
*   11:30 – 12:30: Lunch Break
*   12:30 – 17:00: Initial Coding: Database schema design and environment setup

**Tuesday: Core Development**
*   09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
*   09:30 – 12:30: Frontend Development: Building UI components and wireframes
*   12:30 – 13:30: Lunch Break
*   13:30 – 17:00: Backend Development: Developing API endpoints and server-side logic

**Wednesday: Integration & Unit Testing**
*   09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
*   09:30 – 12:30: Feature Integration: Connecting frontend components to backend services
*   12:30 – 13:30: Lunch Break
*   13:30 – 15:30: Unit Testing: Writing and executing automated test scripts
*   15:30 – 17:00: Peer Code Review: Reviewing pull requests and addressing feedback

**Thursday: QA & Bug Fixing**
*   09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
*   09:30 – 12:30: Bug Fixing: Resolving issues identified during Wednesday's testing
*   12:30 – 13:30: Lunch Break
*   13:30 – 17:00: Quality Assurance (QA): Manual regression testing and performance checks

**Friday: Final Polish & Review**
*   09:00 – 09:30: Daily Stand-up
*   09:30 – 12:00: Final Polish: UI refinements and technical documentation updates
*   12:00 – 13:00: Lunch Break
*   13:00 – 15:00: Team Demo: Presenting the completed feature to stakeholders
*   15:00 – 17:00: Weekly Retrospective: Reviewing progress and planning for the next sprint<turn|>
